ai.headline: FBI Exploits Push Notifications for Surveillance
ai.body: The FBI has found a way to hijack push notifications on people's devices, allowing them to monitor and gather information without users' knowledge or consent. This revelation raises serious concerns about privacy and the government's ability to conduct surveillance through everyday technology. Experts warn that this tactic undermines the security and trust that people place in their devices and apps, and call for greater transparency and accountability from law enforcement agencies.

sources.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI

ai.headline: Artemis II Mission Ends, NASA Faces Uncertain Future
ai.body: The Artemis II mission, which sent four astronauts on a daring journey around the Moon, has safely returned to Earth. However, the successful completion of this mission raises questions about NASA's next steps. With the Artemis program facing budget constraints and technical challenges, the space agency must now determine its priorities and chart a course forward that balances ambition with pragmatism. Experts suggest that NASA may need to reevaluate its long-term goals and explore alternative approaches to achieve its objectives.

sources.title: The Artemis II mission has ended. Where does NASA go from here?
Four astronauts are back home after a daring ride around the Moon

ai.headline: Californians Sue Over AI Tool Recording Doctor Visits
ai.body: A group of Californians have filed a lawsuit against a healthcare provider for using an AI tool that secretly records patient-doctor conversations. The plaintiffs argue that this practice violates their privacy and undermines the trust and confidentiality that should exist in the doctor-patient relationship. The case highlights the growing concerns around the use of AI in sensitive healthcare settings and the need for stronger regulations to protect individuals' rights and personal information.

sources.title: Californians sue over AI tool that records doctor visits

ai.headline: Anthropic Bans OpenClaw Creator from Accessing Claude
ai.body: Anthropic, the AI research company, has temporarily banned the creator of the OpenClaw project from accessing its language model, Claude. This move comes after the creator allegedly used the model in ways that Anthropic deemed inappropriate or unauthorized. The incident raises questions about the control and oversight that AI companies have over their models, and the potential tensions that can arise between researchers and the companies developing these powerful technologies.

sources.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude

btc.headline: Bhutan Dumps 70% of Bitcoin Holdings in 18 Months
btc.body: The tiny Himalayan nation of Bhutan has sold off 70% of its Bitcoin holdings over the past 18 months, according to a report from CoinDesk. The country may have also halted its Bitcoin mining operations. Bhutan's central bank, the Royal Monetary Authority, has not provided a clear explanation for the sell-off, leading to speculation that the country is scaling back its crypto ambitions. The news comes as a surprise, as Bhutan had been seen as a rare example of a government embracing Bitcoin and exploring its potential use cases.

sources.title: Bhutan has sold 70% of its bitcoin in 18 months. It may have stopped BTC mining too.

food.name: Moroccan Lamb Tagine
food.description: Slow-cooked lamb in a fragrant blend of spices, dried fruits, and vegetables, served over fluffy couscous.
food.tip: Let the tagine simmer for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld and the meat to become tender.

exercise.name: Yoga Flow for Strength and Flexibility
exercise.description: A dynamic yoga sequence that combines challenging poses to build strength and improve overall flexibility.

learn.description: Explore the fascinating evolution of cryptography, from ancient ciphers to modern-day encryption techniques.
learn.why: Understanding the history of cryptography provides valuable insights into the ongoing battle between code-makers and code-breakers, and how it has shaped our digital world.
